subject: Chemistry
q: Consider the species CH4, NH4+ and BH4-. Choose the correct option with respect to the there species:
question: Consider the species CH4, NH4+ and BH4-. Choose the correct option with respect to the there species:
options: They are isoelectronic and only two have tetrahedral structures
They are isoelectronic and all have tetrahedral structures
Only two are isoelectronic and all have tetrahedral structures
Only two are isoelectronic and only two have tetrahedral structures
solution:
In CH4, NH4+, BH4- the number of electrons are 10 for each.

Hence, all the three species given are isoelectronic, and the central atom shows sp3 hybridization. Hence, they have tetrahedral structure.

subject: Chemistry
q: The covalent radii of atoms A and B are $r_A$ and $r_B$, respectively. The covalent bond length and total length of AB molecule are respectively
question: The covalent radii of atoms A and B are $r_A$ and $r_B$, respectively. The covalent bond length and total length of AB molecule are respectively
solution:
The covalent bond length of the AB molecule is the internuclear distance between atom A and atom B.

Bond length = $r_A + r_B$

The total length of the AB molecule is the distance between the outermost boundaries of the two atoms along the internuclear axis.

Total length = Radius of A + Bond length + Radius of B

Total length = $r_A + (r_A + r_B) + r_B = 2(r_A + r_B)$

Answer: $(r_A + r_B), 2(r_A + r_B)$

q: Arrange the bonds in order of increasing ionic character in the molecules. <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>LiF</mi><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">K</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">N</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><msub><mi>SO</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>ClF</mi><mn>3</mn></msub><mo>.</mo></math>
question: Arrange the bonds in order of increasing ionic character in the molecules. <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>LiF</mi><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">K</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">N</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><msub><mi>SO</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>ClF</mi><mn>3</mn></msub><mo>.</mo></math>
solution:
The ionic character in a molecule is dependent upon the electronegativity difference between the constituting atoms. The greater the difference, the greater will be the ionic character of the molecule.

N2<SO2<ClF3<K2O<LiF

The Ionic character depends upon the difference in electronegativity (bond polarity).

N2 is a covalent molecule & the difference in electrongegativities are:
|XS – X0| = 1.0
|XCl – XF| =1.0
|XK – X0| =2.6
|XLi – XF| =3.5

subject: Chemistry
q:
$SF_4$ is isostructural with:
A. $BrF_4^{\ominus}$
B. $CH_4$
C. $IF_4^{\oplus}$
D. $XeF_4$
E. $XeO_2 F_2$
Choose the correct answer from the options given below:
question:
$SF_4$ is isostructural with:
A. $BrF_4^{\ominus}$
B. $CH_4$
C. $IF_4^{\oplus}$
D. $XeF_4$
E. $XeO_2 F_2$
Choose the correct answer from the options given below:
solution:
In $SF_4$, the central sulphur atom has $6$ valence electrons. It forms $4$ sigma bonds with fluorine atoms and has $1$ lone pair. The steric number is $5$, corresponding to $sp^3d$ hybridization, and the shape is see-saw.

In $BrF_4^{\ominus}$, the central bromine atom has $8$ valence electrons ($7 + 1$). It forms $4$ sigma bonds and has $2$ lone pairs. The steric number is $6$, corresponding to $sp^3d^2$ hybridization, and the shape is square planar.

In $CH_4$, the central carbon atom has $4$ valence electrons. It forms $4$ sigma bonds and has $0$ lone pairs. The steric number is $4$, corresponding to $sp^3$ hybridization, and the shape is tetrahedral.

In $IF_4^{\oplus}$, the central iodine atom has $6$ valence electrons ($7 - 1$). It forms $4$ sigma bonds and has $1$ lone pair. The steric number is $5$, corresponding to $sp^3d$ hybridization, and the shape is see-saw.

In $XeF_4$, the central xenon atom has $8$ valence electrons. It forms $4$ sigma bonds and has $2$ lone pairs. The steric number is $6$, corresponding to $sp^3d^2$ hybridization, and the shape is square planar.

In $XeO_2F_2$, the central xenon atom has $8$ valence electrons. It forms $4$ sigma bonds ($2$ with oxygen, $2$ with fluorine), $2$ pi bonds, and has $1$ lone pair. The steric number is $5$, corresponding to $sp^3d$ hybridization, and the shape is see-saw.

Therefore, $IF_4^{\oplus}$ and $XeO_2F_2$ are isostructural with $SF_4$.

Answer: C and E Only

q: The number of bent-shaped molecule/s from the following is ______ <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msubsup><mi mathvariant="normal">N</mi><mn>3</mn><mo>-</mo></msubsup><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><msubsup><mi>NO</mi><mn>2</mn><mo>-</mo></msubsup><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><msubsup><mi mathvariant="normal">I</mi><mn>3</mn><mo>-</mo></msubsup><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi><mn>3</mn></msub><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><msub><mi>SO</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></math>
question: The number of bent-shaped molecule/s from the following is ______ <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msubsup><mi mathvariant="normal">N</mi><mn>3</mn><mo>-</mo></msubsup><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><msubsup><mi>NO</mi><mn>2</mn><mo>-</mo></msubsup><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><msubsup><mi mathvariant="normal">I</mi><mn>3</mn><mo>-</mo></msubsup><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi><mn>3</mn></msub><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><msub><mi>SO</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></math>
solution:
N3-Azide: N¯=N+=N¯↔N¯-N+≡N

It has sp hybridised central atom. Hence it is linear in shape.

I3-(triiodide):

                          

It has linear geometry, sp3d hybridisation with three lone pairs at central atom.

NO2- (nitrite) :  

It is (nonlinear) bent shaped as it has sp2 hybridisation with one lone pair at central atom.

O3 (ozone) :  

It Is bent in shape with sp2 hybridisation and one lone pair at central atom.

SO2(sulphur dioxide) : 

                    

It is bent in shape with sp2 hybridisation and one lone pair at central atom.

So 3 among the given molecules is bent in shape.

subject: Chemistry
q:
Consider the following molecules and statements related to them:

(a) (B) is more likely to be crystaline than (A)

(b) (B) has higher boiling point than (A)

(c) (B) dissolves more readily than (A) in water

Identify the correct option from below:
question:
Consider the following molecules and statements related to them:

(a) (B) is more likely to be crystaline than (A)

(b) (B) has higher boiling point than (A)

(c) (B) dissolves more readily than (A) in water

Identify the correct option from below:
solution:
The molecules of B are joined by intermolecular hydrogen bonding and molecules of A associated through dipole-dipole interactions.Hence, the B is more likely to be crystalline than A. Due to inter molecularH-Bonding in B, than A, B is more soluble and having more boiling point than A.

*Note: All the three statements given in the question are correct but the given answer according to NTA, is statements (b) and (c).

solution:
Two l.p. on central atom is =ClF3,XeF4

The valence shell electron pair repulsion (VSEPR) theory is a model used to predict 3-D molecular geometry based on the number of valence shell electron bond pairs among the atoms in a molecule or ion. This model assumes that electron pairs will arrange themselves to minimize repulsion effects from one another.

subject: Chemistry
q: The sum of number of lone pairs of electrons present on the central atoms of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>XeO</mi><mn>3</mn></msub><mo>,</mo><msub><mi>XeOF</mi><mn>4</mn></msub></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>XeF</mi><mn>6</mn></msub></math> is
question: The sum of number of lone pairs of electrons present on the central atoms of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>XeO</mi><mn>3</mn></msub><mo>,</mo><msub><mi>XeOF</mi><mn>4</mn></msub></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>XeF</mi><mn>6</mn></msub></math> is
solution:
Number of lone pairs on the central atom=Number of hybrid orbitals of the central atom-Number of sigma bonds of the central atom.

Number of hybrid orbitals or

Hybridisation for a molecule is given by  = 12V+H-C+A

Where V= Number of valance electrons in central atom.

H = Number of surrounding monovalent atoms.

C=  Cationic charge

A = Anionic charge.

XeF6: 

Number of hybrid orbitals are 7.

Number of sigma bonds formed by the central atom are 6

Number of lone pairs on the central atom=7-6=1 lone pair

XeOF4:8+42=6

Number of hybrid orbitals are 6

Number of sigma bonds formed by the central atom are 5

Number of lone pairs on the central atom=6-5=1

XeO3:

Number of hybrid orbitals=4

Number of sigma bonds formed by the central atom are 3

Number of lone pairs on the central atom=4-3=1

Hence, the total number of lone pairs on the central atom of XeO3,XeOF4 and XeF6 are 3.

subject: Chemistry
q: The formation of molecular complex $\mathrm{BF}_3-\mathrm{NH}_3$ results in a change in hybridization of boron
question: The formation of molecular complex $\mathrm{BF}_3-\mathrm{NH}_3$ results in a change in hybridization of boron
solution: $\operatorname{In~} \mathrm{BF}_3, \mathrm{~B}$ is $s p^2$ hybridized with one empty $p_{\mathrm{z}}$ orbital. The empty $p_{\mathrm{z}}$ orbital of $\mathrm{BF}_3$ can be filled by lone pair of molecules such as $\mathrm{NH}_3$. When this occurs a tetrahedral molecule or ion is formed which is $s p^3$ hybridized.

subject: Chemistry
q: The total number of anti bonding molecular orbitals, formed from <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>2</mn><mi>s</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>2</mn><mi>p</mi></math> atomic orbitals in a diatomic molecule is _____________.
question: The total number of anti bonding molecular orbitals, formed from <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>2</mn><mi>s</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>2</mn><mi>p</mi></math> atomic orbitals in a diatomic molecule is _____________.
solution:
The atomic orbitals combine to form a new set of equivalent orbitals known as hybrid orbitals. Unlike pure orbitals, hybrid orbitals are used in bond formation. The phenomenon is known as hybridisation, which can be defined as the process of intermixing of the orbitals of slightly different energies to redistribute their energies, resulting in the formation of a new set of orbitals of equivalent energies and shape.

The number of hybrid orbitals is equal to the number of atomic orbitals that get hybridised.

Antibonding molecular orbital from 2s=1 that is σ*2s

Antibonding molecular orbital from 2p=3 that is σ*2px,π*2py and π*2pz.

Total =4

subject: Chemistry
q: Which of the following conversions involves change in both shape and hybridisation?
question: Which of the following conversions involves change in both shape and hybridisation?
solution:
Hybridization in Chemistry is defined as the concept of mixing two atomic orbitals to give rise to a new type of hybridized orbitals. This intermixing usually results in the formation of hybrid orbitals having entirely different energies, shapes, etc. The atomic orbitals of the same energy level mainly take part in hybridization. However, both fully filled and half-filled orbitals can also take part in this process, provided they have equal energy.

Here, BF3 has sp2 hybridisation while BF4- has sp3 hybridisation.

subject: Chemistry
q:
Order of Covalent bond;

A. KF>KI; LiF>KF

B. KF<KI; LiF>KF

C. SnCl4>SnCl2; CuCl>NaCl

D. LiF>KF; CuCl>NaCl

E. KF<KI; CuCl>NaCl
question:
Order of Covalent bond;

A. KF>KI; LiF>KF

B. KF<KI; LiF>KF

C. SnCl4>SnCl2; CuCl>NaCl

D. LiF>KF; CuCl>NaCl

E. KF<KI; CuCl>NaCl
solution:
According to Fajan's Rule, small cation has more polarising power and larger anion has more polarisability. These small cations and larger anions favours covalent character.

A. KF>KI-False (Iodide is larger anion than fluoride)

 LiF>KF-True (Lithium-ion is smaller cation than potassium ion)

B. KF<KI-True; LiF>KF-True

C. SnCl4  >SnCl2-True(Sn4+ is smaller than Sn2+); 

The ions with pseudo inert gas configuration prefers the covalent character.

CuCl>NaCl-True(Cu+(3s23p63d10) ion has pseudo inert gas configuration)

D. LiF>KF-True; CuCl<NaCl-False

E. KF<KI-True; CuCl>NaCl-True

subject: Chemistry
q: Identify the molecule $(X)$ with maximum number of lone pairs of electrons (obtained using Lewis dot structure) among $\mathrm{HNO}_{3}, \mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{SO}_{4}, \mathrm{NF}_{3}$ and $\mathrm{O}_{3}$. Choose the correct bond angle made by the central atom of the molecule $(\mathrm{X})$.
question: Identify the molecule $(X)$ with maximum number of lone pairs of electrons (obtained using Lewis dot structure) among $\mathrm{HNO}_{3}, \mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{SO}_{4}, \mathrm{NF}_{3}$ and $\mathrm{O}_{3}$. Choose the correct bond angle made by the central atom of the molecule $(\mathrm{X})$.
solution:
Analyzing lone pairs on central atoms:
HNO₃: N is central with 3 bonds and 0 lone pairs
H₂SO₄: S is central with 4 bonds and 0 lone pairs
NF₃: N is central with 3 bonds and 1 lone pair
O₃: Central O with 2 bonding regions and 1 lone pair
NF₃ has maximum lone pairs (1) with the clearest structure. N undergoes sp³ hybridization with 1 lone pair and 3 bonding pairs giving trigonal pyramidal geometry. The F-N-F bond angle is reduced from ideal tetrahedral 109.5° due to lone pair repulsion, resulting in approximately 102°.

subject: Chemistry
q: In which of the following ionization processes, the bond order has increased and the magnetic behaviour has changed?
question: In which of the following ionization processes, the bond order has increased and the magnetic behaviour has changed?
solution:
In $\mathrm{C}_2-\mathrm{C}_2{ }^{+}$electron is removed from bonding molecular orbital so bond order decreases. In $\mathrm{NO}$ $\longrightarrow \mathrm{NO}^{+}$, electron is removed from anti bonding molecular orbital so bond order increases and nature changes from paramagnetic to diamagnetic. 
Hence, (B) is correct.

subject: Chemistry
q: Choose the polar molecule from the following :
question: Choose the polar molecule from the following :
solution:
The solvents having an appreciable net dipole moment are known as polar solvents.

CHCl3 is polar due the presence of a net dipole moment. There is a difference in electronegativity of the central atom, carbon, hydrogen, and chlorine. This will create a dipole in the molecule. As a dipole has formed in chloroform molecule, due to electronegativity difference, which makes it polar in nature.

Hence, option D is the answer.

q: The shape/structure of<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mfenced close="]" open="["><msub><mi>XeF</mi><mn>5</mn></msub></mfenced><mo>-</mo></msup></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>XeO</mi><mn>3</mn></msub><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">F</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></math>, respectively are :
question: The shape/structure of<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mfenced close="]" open="["><msub><mi>XeF</mi><mn>5</mn></msub></mfenced><mo>-</mo></msup></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>XeO</mi><mn>3</mn></msub><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">F</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></math>, respectively are :
solution:
(i) XeF5-
St. No. =(5+2)=7 so hybridisation is=sp3d3 and structure is pentagonal planar.


(ii) XeO3F2   St. No. =5
so hybridisation is =sp3d and structure is trigonal bipyramidal.

subject: Chemistry
q: A molecule with the formula \(\mathrm{AX}_4 \mathrm{Y}\) has all it's elements from p-block. Element A is rarest, monoatomic, non-radioactive from its group and has the lowest ionization enthalpy value among A, X and Y. Elements X and Y have first and second highest electronegativity values respectively among all the known elements. The shape of the molecule is :
question: A molecule with the formula \(\mathrm{AX}_4 \mathrm{Y}\) has all it's elements from p-block. Element A is rarest, monoatomic, non-radioactive from its group and has the lowest ionization enthalpy value among A, X and Y. Elements X and Y have first and second highest electronegativity values respectively among all the known elements. The shape of the molecule is :
solution:
Given A is rarest, monoatomic, non-radioactive p-block element and form \(\mathrm{AX}_4 \mathrm{Y}\) type of molecule.
\(\therefore\) It is concluded that it is Xe
It is given the electronegativity of A is less than X & Y
It is given the electronegativity of \(\mathrm{X} ~\&~ \mathrm{Y}\) is highest and second highest respectively among all element.
\(\therefore \mathrm{X} ~\&~ \mathrm{Y}\) are \(\mathrm{F} ~\&~ \mathrm{O}\)
\(\therefore\) Compound is consider as \(\mathrm{XeOF}_4\) with square pyramidal shape.

subject: Chemistry
q: Which one of the following has the regular tetrahedral structure?
question: Which one of the following has the regular tetrahedral structure?
solution:
Total no of valence electrons $=3+7 \times 4+1=32$
Total No of hybrid orbital $=4$
$\because$ Hybridisation $=\mathrm{sp}^3$
